external://cubrid/src/transaction/log_ma.key_insight: log_sysop_*() is the public C API exposing the sysop primitive — a nested-transaction-within-a-transaction unit that commits/aborts independently of its parent. 18 functions: 9 public (start/start_atomic/commit/abort/attach_to_outer/end_logical_undo/end_logical_compensate/end_logical_run_postpone/end_recovery_postpone) + 9 static helpers (end_type_string/end_random_exit/end_begin/end_unstack/end_final/commit_internal/get_level/get_tran_index_and_tdes/do_postpone). The end record LOG_SYSOP_END (rectype 20) carries one of six LOG_SYSOP_END_TYPE subtypes — COMMIT (default), ABORT, LOGICAL_UNDO, LOGICAL_MVCC_UNDO, LOGICAL_COMPENSATE, LOGICAL_RUN_POSTPONE — switching the union body of LOG_REC_SYSOP_END accordingly. Atomic sysops emit a separate LOG_SYSOP_ATOMIC_START marker (rectype 50) at start so recovery can roll them back eagerly before the redo phase advances. Postpones of a sysop are bracketed by LOG_SYSOP_START_POSTPONE (rectype 18) which carries the eventual end record + first postpone LSA so recovery can resume mid-replay. The `lock_topop` mutex serialises same-tdes sysop lifecycle (relevant only for vacuum workers and other multi-thread-per-tdes contexts). VACUUM integration is centralised: log_sysop_get_tran_index_and_tdes routes vacuum workers to their thread-special TDES via LOG_FIND_THREAD_TRAN_INDEX, so all 18 functions transparently operate on the right tdes without per-callsite branching. Empty-body sysops short-circuit at commit (no LOG_SYSOP_END emitted); the same shortcut is FORBIDDEN for logical end variants — they assert that the body contains at least one log record (workaround: emit a dummy log record). Outermost sysop's end-final is the natural checkpoint trigger point (SERVER_MODE wakes the checkpoint daemon; SA_MODE runs logpb_checkpoint synchronously — but only when not nested, since checkpoint can clear tdes).

external://cubrid/.claude/knowledge/pr_7.no_baseline_bump_reason: PR #7062 still OPEN; per protocol, no PR-reconciliation and no bump until merge.
external://cubrid/.claude/knowledge/pr_7.key_insight: Re-anchored the PR page from review-doc snapshot c28c5945a to live HEAD 0f8a107bb. Major post-snapshot semantic deltas: (1) system parameter de-unified — `parallel_scan_page_threshold` (server, 2048) and `parallel_index_scan_page_threshold` (client/session, 32) split into two axes (commit 8dc998587), (2) 137-line cost-gate `qo_apply_parallel_index_scan_threshold` in plan_generation.c performs `metric = ceil(sel * leaf_pages)` selectivity-based degree decision (formula `floor(log2(metric/threshold))+2`), (3) latch-couple introduced on both root→leaf descent and leaf-chain advance in commit 0f8a107bb, narrowing the mid-iteration B+tree split race window, (4) double-fix removed in both list-scan (ddf2e3374) and index-scan (1dd52e1b8), halving page-fix counts on the hot loop, (5) lazy CAS membuf claim (m_membuf_claimed.compare_exchange_strong) replaces static idx==0 worker binding, fixing the silent-skip when idx-0 worker fails before loop entry, (6) BUILDVALUE_OPT whitelist confirmed at 13 aggregates (was off-by-two as 11 in prior wiki page), (7) qfile_collect_list_sector_info reused from PR #6981 with per-page tfile tracking. None of these introduce new wiki surface beyond the existing Reconciliation Plan; all are absorbed into existing plan items + the branch-WIP parallel-list-scan companion page.

Three baseline-truth incidentals: list-file gets membuf-only-on-base + qfile_close_list contract + page-header-has-no-writer-marker; file-manager gets temp-vs-permanent FTAB layout (load-bearing for parallel temp scans); parallel-query-task gets the list_id save/restore stack-local dance. PR-7062 page flagged its own pre-`0f8a107bb` claims as superseded.
